situation: audit-2026-07-07-047 finding SZ-SH-H-001 (severity=high, kind=route-fix): manifest.json grants host_permissions=['<all_urls>'] but extension has zero content_scripts, zero executeScript/insertCSS calls, and all fetch() targets are external APIs (Anthropic Vision, OCR upload) which MV3 service workers can reach without host_permissions. Only chrome.tabs.query usage is in popup for active tab — requires no host permission. Excess <all_urls> violates Chrome Web Store minimum-privilege policy and expands attack surface if any future code path leaks.
action: Removed 'host_permissions' field entirely from shizi/manifest.json. Verified no content_scripts, executeScript, insertCSS, or webRequest usage in src/. Confirmed chrome.tabs.query({active:true,currentWindow:true}) needs only 'activeTab' permission (already listed). Committed via opt-worktree.sh to auto/opt-shizi-1783404582 (route-fix reuse of existing shizi:permissions worktree). Pushed to origin.
outcome_evidence: Commit ddef10f on auto/opt-shizi-1783404582 pushed to origin/auto/opt-shizi-1783404582. git diff shows host_permissions line removed. No other files touched.
next_suggestion: SZ-BG-M-003: compressFallback/preprocessForVL O(n²) base64 concat — same pattern as merged SZ-BG-H-002, apply ArrayBuffer fix
SZ-BG-M-005: mergeMultiSourceResults lacks skipFuzzy perf guard — O(n²) editDistance on large region sets
SZ-BG-M-007: Vision API fetch calls have no timeout — can hang indefinitely

audit_findings.finding: host_permissions grants <all_urls> but extension never uses tab/scripting APIs requiring it
audit_findings.remediation: Remove host_permissions field entirely; activeTab permission suffices for chrome.tabs.query
